features.name: Initialize repository and publish markdown docs
features.description: Initialize the local project as a Git repository and push the markdown project documents to GitHub.
evidence: Created init.sh as the standard smoke-test entry point.
Created feature_list.json as the feature status source of truth.
PowerShell-equivalent smoke test passed because this Windows host only exposes the WSL bash shim and has no installed WSL distribution.
Merged remote main history without file conflicts.
Pushed local markdown documentation and project metadata to origin/main.

features.name: Create project README
features.description: Create a repository README that summarizes the project purpose, architecture, feature modules, document structure, and validation entry point.
evidence: Created README.md based on PRD.md and 系统设计说明书.md.
Smoke test passed after README creation.
README update was pushed to origin/main.

features.name: Task C3 fire and smoke detection plugin
features.description: Implement the YOLO-backed fire/smoke detector plugin with 30-frame confidence-window debouncing and AlarmEvent(type=fire_smoke) output.
required_validation: python -m pytest tests/test_intrusion.py tests/test_fight.py tests/test_fight_integration.py tests/test_face.py tests/test_alarm_center.py tests/test_fire_smoke.py
Real fire/smoke YOLO weight and video validation
evidence: Implemented backend/app/detectors/fire_smoke.py with FireSmokeDetector.feed() preserving FIRE_WINDOW/FIRE_CONF sliding-window logic.

Added FireSmokePlugin(Detector) with setup() YOLO weight loading, fire/smoke class filtering, max-confidence extraction, and AlarmEvent(type=fire_smoke) output including camera_id, ts, snapshot, confidence, and extra metadata.
Registered FireSmokePlugin in backend/run.py so it is managed by InferenceEngine without creating detector-local threads or loops.
Added backend/tests/test_fire_smoke.py covering full-window debounce, single-frame flash rejection, fire/smoke class acceptance, non-fire/smoke filtering, and empty-weight rejection.
pytest passed: tests/test_fire_smoke.py -> 6 passed.
Backend related pytest passed: 38 passed for intrusion, fight, fight integration, face, alarm center, and fire smoke tests.
Backend smoke test passed after restoring merge-damaged foundation pieces.
PowerShell-equivalent init smoke passed with 15 markdown docs.
blockers: backend/model_weights/fire_smoke.pt is currently a 0-byte placeholder. Real YOLO/video validation requires replacing it with a trained non-empty fire/smoke weight file.

features.name: Task E alarm center and DingTalk close loop
features.description: Implement the unified alarm event contract, alarm persistence/snapshot/dedup service, alarm WebSocket push, DingTalk primary/escalation workflow, and alarm query/confirm APIs.
required_validation: python -m pytest tests/test_intrusion.py tests/test_fight.py tests/test_fight_integration.py tests/test_face.py tests/test_alarm_center.py
evidence: Updated AlarmEvent with type, region_id, camera_id, ts, level, snapshot_url, face_match, extra, while preserving legacy confidence/snapshot compatibility.

Implemented AlarmService raise_alarm() with 30s region/type dedup, snapshot persistence, intrusion/occupy face matching fallback, alarm_event persistence, level=0 private-only handling, WebSocket broadcast, and DingTalk notification trigger.
Implemented /ws/alarms broadcast registration and /api/alarms query, confirm, and snapshot access endpoints.
Implemented DingTalkNotifier notify/confirm/escalate with notification_log primary/escalated records, status transitions, timer cancellation, and no-webhook local-safe behavior.
Extended ORM models for camera_id, fight alarm type, extra JSON, Guard, notification_log guard FK, and indexes.
Added backend/tests/test_alarm_center.py covering persistence/snapshot/notify, dedup, level=0 private-only behavior, DingTalk confirm/escalation logs, and alarm HTTP API.
pytest passed: 27 passed for intrusion, fight, fight integration, face, and alarm center tests.

Real MySQL verification passed against DATABASE_URI from .env: connected to study_room, seeded minimal camera/region/guards, wrote confirmed fight, escalated intrusion, private fatigue alarms, and verified DingTalk notification logs without exposing secrets.
Added backend/scripts/verify_task_e_real_db.py as the repeatable real-database verification entry point.
backend app config now loads .env automatically and supports both KEY= and PowerShell $env:KEY= syntax.
backend/requirements.txt includes PyMySQL for mysql+pymysql DATABASE_URI values.
backend/tests/smoke_test.py passed after restoring StreamScheduler ring_buffer maxlen to 5.
PowerShell-equivalent init smoke passed with 16 markdown docs.
Real DingTalk text webhook connectivity was verified with errcode=0 after DINGTALK_WEBHOOK was added to .env.
Added DingTalk signed webhook support via DINGTALK_SECRET and DINGTALK_LEADER_SECRET, plus public ActionCard confirm/snapshot URLs via PUBLIC_BASE_URL.
Added GET /api/alarms/{id}/confirm for DingTalk ActionCard button confirmation while preserving the existing POST confirm API.
Regression after DingTalk real-integration prep passed: 28 task-E-related tests, backend smoke test with ALL SMOKE TESTS PASSED, and real MySQL verify script.
Real DingTalk ActionCard smoke send through DingTalkNotifier returned errcode=0; click-confirm still awaits PUBLIC_BASE_URL.
After pulling origin/main on 2026-07-09, removed duplicate ws.register_ws_routes(sock) registration in backend/app/__init__.py and verified python tests/smoke_test.py passed again.

Post-merge validation repaired duplicate WebSocket registration, duplicate Guard ORM definition, missing AlarmService helper methods, and FaceMatcher immediate AlarmEvent compatibility; validation passed with bash ./init.sh, 39 pytest cases including fire_smoke, smoke test, and real MySQL verify.

Real DingTalk close-loop send test passed on 2026-07-09: primary ActionCard returned errcode=0, a 5-second unconfirmed timer escalated the alarm to status=escalated/level=2, and the escalated ActionCard also returned errcode=0.
The real escalation test wrote notification_log rows for guard role primary and guard role leader, confirming DB-level guard selection for primary/escalated stages.

Fixed local DingTalk confirm-button testing by changing .env PUBLIC_BASE_URL from an RTMP stream URL to http://127.0.0.1:5000; a new ActionCard for alarm 16 was sent successfully with an HTTP confirm URL.

Enhanced DingTalk alarm cards to show actor, behavior, alarm type, handler, location, camera, snapshot, and score context; added a separate text @ message to mention the selected guard by guard.dingtalk_id.
Real DingTalk actor/handler smoke passed for alarm 17: ActionCard returned errcode=0 with HTTP confirm URL and the follow-up text message with at payload also returned errcode=0.
blockers: bash ./init.sh now passes after Ubuntu WSL was installed; WSL still prints a localhost proxy warning in NAT mode.
Full DingTalk ActionCard click-confirm verification still needs PUBLIC_BASE_URL pointing at a public tunnel to the backend.
DINGTALK_LEADER_WEBHOOK is not configured, so escalation currently reuses the primary group robot; true separate leader-group delivery needs a leader webhook.
The @ mention payload is now sent, but real human notification still depends on guard.dingtalk_id being a DingTalk user ID or mobile accepted by the robot.
